content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

April 20, 1982


Baron Reed
Deputy Director
Department of Boating
& Waterways
1416 Ninth Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice Letter No. 82-062

Dear Mr. Reed:

Thank you for your request for written advice. The
facts as stated in your letter are as follows:

You are a Deputy Director of the Department of Boating
and Waterways, which is an exempt position. You have filed
a Declaration of Candidacy for the 14th Congressional District.
You intend to take a leave of absence effective April 30,
1982, to campaign full time for this position through the
primary election on June 8, 1982.

You ask "whether there is a legal conflict between my
plans and the Political Reform Act." As I advised you over
the telephone, there is no conflict which arises from these
facts. As a federal candidate, you do not come within the
coverage of the Political Reform Act.

If you have further questions, please do not hesitate
to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. LeIdigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh

State of California

Memorandum

FPPC

To : Robert Leidigh
Fair Political Practices
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Resources Agency of California

Date : March 18, 1982

Subject :

From : Department of Boating and Waterways

Dear Robert:

As we discussed on the telephone recently, I am an exempt Deputy Director
at the Department of Boating and Waterways and have filed a Declaration of
Candidacy for the 14th Congressional District.

I intend to take a leave of absence effective April 30, 1982 to campaign full
time for this position through the primary election on June 8, 1982. Would you
please provide me with informal advise as to whether there is a legal conflict
between my plans and the Political Reform Act. Thank you in advance for your
assistance.

Sincerely,

BARON REED
Deputy Director

sections.question: Whether there is a legal conflict between my plans and the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: As a federal candidate, you do not come within the coverage of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a state official who is filing as a federal candidate and taking a leave of absence to campaign create a conflict under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict exists because federal candidates are not covered by the California Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format. Question and conclusion are clearly present in body text. Federal candidate status and lack of PRA coverage are key legal determinations. Minor formatting variations in signature block.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether there is a legal conflict between my plans and the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: As a federal candidate, you do not come within the coverage of the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises a Deputy Director at the Department of Boating and Waterways that there is no Political Reform Act conflict arising from filing as a federal candidate for the 14th Congressional District and taking a leave of absence to campaign full-time.
